experiences.intentions: Board surface is low and fairly centered, with a T piece spawning near the top. The stack has a central O over Z over I over S pattern, leaving some unevenness around the middle. Goal is to place the T to improve the center without creating overhangs; immediate sub-goal is to get it down quickly into the middle region where T can help fill contours. Horizontal spawn orientation is already reasonable, and moving/rotating now is premature without seeing lower interactions; hard drop risks a poor top placement because the piece is still at spawn height. Soft dropping advances toward placement safely while preserving future adjustment options.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty above a compact stack centered near the bottom. The active T is high and has plenty of time; there are no immediate threats. The surface around the stack suggests keeping flexibility and setting the T flat is reasonable. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early far above the stack if current x is not ideal; rotations are not necessary yet; soft_drop just advances downward without improving alignment. Moving left shifts the T toward the center-left, a better lane to eventually place it over the uneven stack and preserve future options with upcoming J and L pieces.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an O at the very top (rows 1-2, cols 3-4), with only one rotation. The stack is low and uneven but there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place the O efficiently to flatten the surface and avoid creating holes. Since O cannot rotate and is high above the stack, the best immediate progress is horizontal positioning. The right side around cols 6-7 has a shallow notch above the current L/J/Z/I structure that an O can help level better than staying near the left. So moving right now advances toward a cleaner placement; dropping immediately from current column would leave a less useful top. Candidates: rotate does nothing, no_op wastes a turn, left shifts away from the better landing zone, soft/hard drop commits too early. Best action: right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I at the very top in horizontal orientation spanning columns 3-6. The stack is uneven with a clear vertical well around column 4-5 areas, and there is no immediate danger at the top yet. Goal is to place the I efficiently, ideally vertically into a gap rather than leave it horizontal near the surface. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it flat on top, which is poor; soft_drop commits downward before aligning; left/right could reposition but vertical orientation looks stronger because rotation 1 appears valid in the current column and can fit a narrow lane. Rotating right now preserves flexibility and sets up a better drop into the central well.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.
